conflict_type: Conflicts between Keyword Constraints and Phrase Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings.
org_constraint: Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule.
new_constraint: Avoid using the word "brainstorming" in the schedule.
instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule. Avoid using the word "brainstorming" in the schedule.
response:
Here is a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature:

## New Feature Development Schedule (Week 1)

**Goal:** Establish foundational structure, complete core logic implementation, and validate initial functionality.

| Day | Morning (9:00 AM - 12:00 PM) | Afternoon (1:00 PM - 5:00 PM) | Focus Areas & Meetings |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Monday** | **Planning & Design Review:** Deep dive into feature requirements and architecture decisions. | **Setup & Scaffolding:** Developers begin setting up necessary repositories, base components, and boilerplate code. | **Daily Standup (9:00 AM):** Quick status update. **Requirement Alignment Meeting (10:30 AM):** Initial technical scoping. |
| **Tuesday** | **Core Feature Development (Sprint Focus 1):** Implementing the primary data handling logic. | **Component Development:** Building out the main UI/UX components related to the feature. | **Daily Standup (9:00 AM).** **Collaborative brainstorming session (2:00 PM):** Reviewing edge cases and potential user flows. |
| **Wednesday** | **Code Implementation (Sprint Focus 2):** Integrating the backend services with the frontend components. | **Unit Testing & Initial QA:** Developers write unit tests; QA begins smoke testing the integrated components. | **Daily Standup (9:00 AM).** **Technical Sync (3:00 PM):** Reviewing integration points and identifying dependencies. |
| **Thursday** | **Refactoring & Optimization:** Reviewing initial code against best practices; performance tuning. | **Feature Testing Cycle:** Dedicated time for QA to execute comprehensive test plans on the built feature. | **Daily Standup (9:00 AM).** **QA Review Session (2:00 PM):** Detailed bug reporting and prioritization. |
| **Friday** | **Bug Fixing & Iteration:** Focused sprint on resolving P1 and P2 bugs identified throughout the week. | **Feature Complete Demonstration & Review:** Team presents the working feature to stakeholders for initial feedback. | **Daily Standup (9:00 AM).** **Retrospective Meeting (4:00 PM):** Discussing what went well and areas for process improvement next week. |

***

**Key Schedule Notes:**

*   **Daily Standup:** 15 minutes maximum to ensure alignment and quickly flag blockers.
*   **Coding Time:** Dedicated blocks for focused implementation, which is adjusted daily based on complexity.
*   **Testing Time:** Dedicated blocks for both developer-led unit testing and formal QA cycles.
*   **Collaborative Input:** The afternoon session on Tuesday is reserved specifically for unstructured, shared idea generation to refine the feature scope organically.<turn|>
